channel.title: Comments for page "Dogs of War"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Dogs of War"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for commenting; I appreciate it (and I'm glad you enjoyed it!)

RE: Skip Weaponization
– hide block

To be fair, this is the Resurrection canon I'm writing in here — 'weaponizing anomalies' is pretty much the entire basis of the setting. That being said, one of my goals here was to write a set of tales regarding weaponizing SCPs that "feels" both entertaining and credible.

Tobias Whyte is an analog to those who romanticize the violence of the past; he's a brilliant but arrogant researcher who's far too young to have experienced the "glory days", yet still insists how great it was when "restraint" meant dropping four nukes instead of five. There's a reason I have him up against Commander Robert Malthus — a man who actually experienced the very days that Tobias glorifies.

Malthus is an old-school Foundation agent — a downright Biblical mother-fucker. He's the kind of monster you call when you need babes strangled in their cribs. But he knows that this is horrible, just as he knows that every act of violence carries a deep and irreversible cost. And the Foundation's ledger is dripping in red. But Tobias?

Tobias is just a little piece of shit.

Your post here reminded me that I've yet to finish the final piece of this work. Real life has been interfering with me a lot recently, but I really want to give cranking out the final part of this a try over the weekend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It was an impressive work, indeed. I upvoted two consecutive "skip weaponization" tales, that is something.

You have good characterization and character development, and the way you developed the SCPs used beyond their original articles was also interesting and believable. I'm not totally on board with 3033, because I'm not a fan of an SCP being a technology the Foundation can actually replicate, but in the context of the story I can put that easily aside.

The 3033 project being put aside was a nice touch, and based on Charles, I even think that your reasoning is similar to mine. Tobias think they have weakened, but he's the one who's actually lost in dreams of the past and can't see the stupidity of his work.

Upvoted again, against my expectations. I do not expect this to repeat itself with the third part, but I want it to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is sheer excellence. Every character stands out amazingly, and the fact that I finally recognize that these are the same things from Mutable State only makes it better. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is gorgeous beyond belief. I love it! Your character development in the doctors, Charles and Queen all come off beautifully and naturally. I'd love to see how this takes off. Deserves a lot more than just a single +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, I really like where this is going. You do great character voices, and now that these characters have all been wound up, I can't wait to see them let go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellent and enjoyable. You gave Smoots a voice that comes right off the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everything about this, as well as the previous parts, just makes me go

W o a h

+1 forever :,) I am so excited for the conclusion

item.title: ruh roh
item.content:encoded:
I'm out of my head,
Of my heart, and my mind,
'Cuz you can run, but you can't hide --
I'm gonna make you mine

Out of my head,
Of my heart, and my mind,
'Cuz I can feel how your flesh, now --
is crying out for

more

- The Wolf, SIAMÉS

AUTHOR NOTES
– hide block

The opening bit is based on that scene from Robocop 2 (warning for 1980s movie gore and existential cyborg horror/suicide).

So, this turned out longer than I expected. The third part (and actual conclusion) will hopefully be done before the weekend is over. We'll see!

Like the article? Click here for more of The Great Hippo's Skippos!
